banners.temporary: This temporary account will expire in <1><0>{{in}}</0></1> and is only accessible from this browser. Sign up now for free to save your settings.
banners.nearQuota: You have made more than {{queriesNearQuota, number}} queries this month, getting close to the free monthly quota of {{queriesQuota, number}} queries. <5>Subscribe</5> to continue using NextDNS.
banners.overQuota: You have made more than {{queriesQuota, number}} queries this month and all features have been disabled. <3>Subscribe</3> to continue using NextDNS.

account.passwordResetInfo: Enter the email address associated with your account and we will send you a link to reset your password.
account.passwordResetLinkSentMessage: A link to reset your password was sent to <1>{{email}}</1>.
account.convertingNotice: You are converting a temporary account to a real account, all your settings will be saved.
account.convertingDeviceOnlyNotice: You are converting a device only account into a full account, all your settings will be saved.

currentPassword.incorrect: The password you entered is incorrect.

affiliation.name: Affiliation
affiliation.description: Help us spread the word by sharing <1>{{link}}</1> and receive <3>{{share, percentage}}</3> of all subscriptions for the first <5>{{months, number}} months</5>.

availableBalance.name: Available balance
availableBalance.description: Balance becomes available to pay out after a month in order to cover potential refunds.

payout.minimum: An available balance of at least {{amount, price}} is needed for payouts.

usage.name: Free usage
usage.description: NextDNS can be used for free up to {{queries, number}} queries each month, after which all features will be disabled temporarily until next month.

overQuota.message: You have made more than {{queries, number}} queries this month.
overQuota.info: NextDNS will continue to answer DNS queries but all features are temporarily disabled until next month.

subscribe.ios: To subscribe to the service, go to my.nextdns.io in your browser.
subscribe.upsell: Get unlimited queries, starting at {{amount, price}}/month.

subscription.name: Subscription
subscription.expires: Your subscription will expire on {{date, date}}.
subscription.managedByApple: Your subscription is managed through your Apple ID. To change or cancel it, please use Apple's subscription settings.
subscription.renewal: Your next automatic payment will be for {{amount, price}} on {{date, date}}.

confirm.text: Your current subscription will stay valid until {{date, date}}.

subscribe.included: All plans include <1>access to all features</1>, <3>unlimited devices</3> and <5>unlimited queries</5>.
subscribe.savings: Save {{percent, percentage}} over the monthly plan
subscribe.familyOnly: * Pro plans are for personal (and close family) use only.

unitsQuestion.business: How many <1>employees</1> does your business employ?
unitsQuestion.education: How many <1>students</1> does your school (or district) have?

asYouGrow.business: * Remember to update your plan as your number of employees grows.
asYouGrow.education: * Remember to update your plan as your number of students grows.

starting.business: <0>Starting at </0><1>{{price, price}}</1><2> per month for {{units, number}} employees</2>
starting.education: <0>Starting at </0><1>{{price, price}}</1><2> per month for {{units, number}} students</2>

card.stripe: We use Stripe — a leading payment gateway — to process card payments in a secure way without ever having access to your card information directly.

paypal.text: Click the Continue to PayPal button to subscribe with PayPal.

bitpay.text: Click the Continue to BitPay button to pay with cryptocurrency.
bitpay.toYearly: We do not accept cryptocurrency payments for monthly plans. <1>By continuing, you will automatically be switched to the yearly plan.</1>
bitpay.remainder: You current subscription is already paid for until {{date, date}}. You will be charged now and the remainder will be automatically added to the new expiration date.

business.name: Business
business.description: For tax purposes, we need your business name, address and VAT number (or equivalent). This is required by law and will appear on all invoices.

errors.required: Enter the name of your business.
Enter the address of your business.

invoices.name: Invoices
invoices.description: Billing history and all invoices for your account.

changeEmail.name: Email
changeEmail.description: Change the email address used to log into your account and receive critical notifications.

changePassword.name: Password
changePassword.description: Change the password used to log into your account and asked when performing sensitive actions.

2fa.name: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
2fa.description: Two-factor authentication (2FA) is an extra layer of security that requires an additional time-based 6-digit code when logging into your account.

modal.howto: Use an authenticator app like <1>Google Authenticator</1> or a password manager supporting 2FA. You can either scan the QR code, tap on it to open the <3>{{otpauth}}</3> link or copy & paste your secret manually.
Use these recovery codes to access your account in the event you lose access to your device and cannot receive two-factor authentication codes. Each code can only be used once.

api.description: Create and manage your profiles programmatically. Read the documentation at <1>{{link}}</1>.

delete.warning: This will delete your account and all your profiles forever.

confirm.text: Are you sure you want to delete your account? This action cannot be undone.

modal.title: Expired
modal.text: Your temporary account has expired. Sign up now to save your settings and continue using NextDNS, it's free.

errors.taken: You already have a profile with that name.

ok.secondary: This device is using NextDNS with this profile.

withoutConfiguration.primary: This device is using NextDNS with no profile.

secondary.clients: Make sure you set the profile ID shown below in the app settings.
secondary.firefox: Make sure you set the DNS-over-HTTPS endpoint shown below in Firefox.
secondary.doh: Make sure you use the DNS-over-HTTPS endpoint shown below.
secondary.dot: Make sure you use the DNS-over-TLS endpoint shown below.
secondary.ipv6: Make sure you use the IPv6 addresses shown below.
secondary.servers: Make sure to use the DNS Servers shown in the Linked IP section below (DNS Servers change between profiles).
secondary.link: Make sure to link your IP address in the Linked IP section below.

withConfiguration.primary: This device is using NextDNS with another profile.

secondary.otherAccount: Make sure you are using one of the endpoints listed on this page.

unconfigured.primary: This device is not using NextDNS.
unconfigured.secondary: This device is currently using ”{{resolver}}” as DNS resolver.

error.primary: Error while checking the current status.
error.secondary: Please talk to us if this problem persists after refreshing the page.

ok.primary: This device is using NextDNS with iCloud Private Relay
ok.secondary: Features like SafeSearch, Youtube Restricted Mode and Rewrites are not compatible with iCloud Private Relay.

blockpage.primary: This device is using NextDNS with iCloud Private Relay.
blockpage.secondary: Enabling the Block Page setting while using iCloud Private Relay interferes with blocking.

endpoints.name: Endpoints
endpoints.description: Set up NextDNS with this profile using one of the endpoints below.
endpoints.help: Not sure how to use those? Follow the <1>Setup Guide</1>.

linkedIp.name: Linked IP
linkedIp.description: If you are unable to set up NextDNS using our apps, DNS-over-TLS, DNS-over-HTTPS or IPv6, then use the DNS servers below and link your IP. This is mostly for use on home networks and not recommended on mobile.

ddns.explanation: You can use Dynamic DNS (DDNS) to have your linked IP updated automatically.
ddns.status: You are currently using <1>{{hostname}}</1> as DDNS hostname, your IP will be automatically updated every minute.

errors.ip: Enter a hostname, not an IP address.
errors.error: There was an error while getting the IP address behind this hostname.
errors.empty: This hostname is not returning any IP address.
errors.multiple: This hostname is returning multiple IP addresses.
errors.different: For security reasons, when adding a Dynamic DNS the IP address returned by the DDNS hostname should be the same as your current IP address.

linkedIp.programmatically: You can also programmatically update your linked IP by calling:

guide.name: Setup Guide
guide.description: Follow the instructions below to set up NextDNS on your device, browser or router.

ipv6.notSupported: Your network does not support IPv6

appleConfigurationProfile.name: NextDNS ({{configuration}})
appleConfigurationProfile.description: This profile enables NextDNS on all networks using the new native Encrypted DNS feature.

steps.1: 1. Go to Settings → Network & internet → Advanced → Private DNS.
steps.2: 2. Select the Private DNS provider hostname option.

steps.1: 1. Install our official app from the Play Store.
steps.2: 2. In the NextDNS app, enter <1>{{id}}</1> in Settings → Configuration ID, then connect.

profile.title: Configuration Profile
profile.step: Use our Apple Configuration Profile Generator available at <1>{{link}}</1>.

steps.1: 1. Install our official app from the App Store.
steps.2: 2. Open the app then go to Settings and toggle "Use Custom Configuration". Enter <1>{{id}}</1> as Configuration ID.

dns-over-https.title: DNS over HTTPS
steps: Click on <1>Hardware properties</1>, or ignore this step if you clicked on <3>Ethernet</3>.
Click the <1>Edit</1> button next to <3>DNS server assignment</3>.
Enter <1>{{ip}}</1> as <3>Preferred DNS</3>, then select <5>On (manual template)</5> and enter <7>{{endpoint}}</7>.
Enter <1>{{ip}}</1> as <3>Alternate DNS</3>, then select <5>On (manual template)</5> and enter <7>{{endpoint}}</7>.

steps.2: 2. After installing, right-click on NextDNS icon in the Systray then open the Settings. Set <1>{{id}}</1> as Configuration ID.
steps.3: 3. Right-click on NextDNS icon in the Systray, then click on Enable.

steps.1: 1. Click on the Start menu, then click on Control Panel.
steps.2: 2. Click on Network and Internet, then Network and Sharing Center.
steps.4: 4. Right click on the Wi-Fi network you are connected to, then click Properties.
steps.7: 7. Click Use The Following DNS Server Addresses.
steps.8: 8. Replace the current addresses (if any) with <1>{{dns1}}</1> and <3>{{dns2}}</3>.
steps.9: 9. Click OK, then Close. You may need to restart your browser.

steps.2: 2. Follow the instructions for NextDNS at <1>{{link}}</1> and use <3>{{id}}</3> as Configuration ID.

steps.1: 1. Install our official app from the Mac App Store.
steps.2: 2. Click on Preferences in the app status bar menu and go to the Configuration tab.
steps.3: 3. Check "Use Custom Configuration" and enter <1>{{id}}</1> as Configuration ID.

steps.1: 1. Open System Preferences, then click on Network.
steps.2: 2. Select the network you are connected to, then click on the Advanced button.
steps.4: 4. In the DNS Servers list, remove all addresses (if any) then add <1>{{dns1}}</1> and <3>{{dns2}}</3>.

cli.title: NextDNS Command-Line Client
cli.manual: Head over to our open-source repository at <1>{{link}}</1> for manual installation instructions.

cli.title: NextDNS Command-Line Client
cli.manual: Head over to our open-source repository at <1>{{link}}</1> for manual installation instructions.

ip.step: Change your DNS servers to <1>{{dns1}}</1> and <3>{{dns2}}</3>.

stubby.warning: Make sure Stubby is linked with <1>OpenSSL 1.1.1 or higher</1> as previous versions will not work with NextDNS.

unbound.warning: As a recursive resolver, Unbound chases CNAMEs. This may result in <1>unexpected behavior</1> when used in conjunction with a blocking DNS resolver like NextDNS. See <3>{{link}}</3>.

ip.step: Change your DNS servers to <1>{{dns1}}</1> and <3>{{dns2}}</3>.

steps.2: 2. In the Privacy and security section, click on Security.
steps.3: 3. In the Advanced section, enable Use secure DNS.
3. Scroll down to Enable DNS over HTTPS.
steps.4: 4. Choose Increased Protection or Max Protection.

steps.2: 2. Go to the Privacy, search, and services section.
steps.3: 3. Under Security, enable Use secure DNS to specify how to lookup the network address for websites.
steps.4: 4. Select Choose a service provider, then enter <1>{{endpoint}}</1>.

steps.2: 2. In the Privacy and security section (under Additional settings), go to Security.
steps.3: 3. In the Advanced section, enable Use secure DNS.

cli.title: NextDNS for Routers
cli.requirements: Only works on routers that can run executables
cli.step: Head over to our open-source repository at <1>{{link}}</1> for installation instructions.

ipv6.warning: Some routers may not support the IPv6 notation above. In that case, use <1>{{dns1}}</1> and <3>{{dns2}}</3>.

steps.1: 1. Open the preferences for your router. Usually you can access it from your browser via a URL (like http://192.168.0.1/ or http://192.168.1.1/).
steps.2: 2. Locate the DNS settings inside the interface.
steps.3: 3. Remove all addresses (if any) then add <1>{{dns1}}</1> and <3>{{dns2}}</3>.

steps.1: 1. Go to Services → DNS Resolver and on the tab General Settings scroll down to the Custom Options box.

tailscale.manual: Head over to the Tailscale documentation at <2>{{link}}</2> for setup instructions.

identify.name: Identify your devices
identify.description: Follow the instructions below to identify your devices in Analytics and Logs.

apps.name: Apps
apps.instructions: Enable "Send Device Name" in the app settings.

dot.instructions: Prepend the name to the provided domain (the name should only contain <1>a-z</1>, <3>A-Z</3>, <5>0-9</5> and <7>-</7>). Use <9>--</9> for spaces.
dot.example: For "<1>John Router</1>", you would use <3>John--Router-{{profile}}.dns.nextdns.io</3> as your DNS-over-TLS endpoint.

doh.instructions: Append the name to the provided URL (the name should be URL encoded).
doh.example: For "<1>John's Firefox</1>", you would use <3>https://dns.nextdns.io/{{profile}}/John's%20Firefox</3> as your DNS-over-HTTPS endpoint.

feeds.name: Threat Intelligence Feeds
feeds.description: Block domains known to distribute malware, launch phishing attacks and host command-and-control servers using a blend of the most reputable threat intelligence feeds — all updated in real-time.

ai.name: AI-Driven Threat Detection
ai.description: Block millions of threats detected by our AI technology — a proprietary AI engine designed from the ground up for DNS with hundreds of signals, terabytes of training data and real-time decision making.

googleSafeBrowsing.name: Google Safe Browsing
googleSafeBrowsing.description: Block malware and phishing domains using Google Safe Browsing — a technology that examines billions of URLs per day looking for unsafe websites. Unlike the version embedded in some browsers, this does not associate your public IP address to threats and does not allow bypassing the block.

cryptojacking.name: Cryptojacking Protection
cryptojacking.description: Prevent the unauthorized use of your devices to mine cryptocurrency.

dnsRebinding.name: DNS Rebinding Protection
dnsRebinding.description: Prevent attackers from taking control of your local devices through the Internet by automatically blocking DNS responses containing private IP addresses.

homograph.name: IDN Homograph Attacks Protection
homograph.description: Block domains that impersonate other domains by abusing the large character set made available with the arrival of Internationalized Domain Names (IDNs) — e.g. replacing the Latin letter "e" with the Cyrillic letter "е".

typosquatting.name: Typosquatting Protection
typosquatting.description: Block domains registered by malicious actors that target users who incorrectly type a website address into their browser — e.g. gooogle.com instead of google.com.

dga.name: Domain Generation Algorithms (DGAs) Protection
dga.description: Block domains generated by Domain Generation Algorithms (DGAs) seen in various families of malware that can be used as rendezvous points with their command and control servers.

nrd.name: Block Newly Registered Domains (NRDs)
nrd.description: Block domains registered less than 30 days ago. Those domains are known to be favored by threat actors to launch malicious campaigns.

ddns.name: Block Dynamic DNS Hostnames
ddns.description: Dynamic DNS (or DDNS) services let malicious actors quickly set up hostnames for free and without any validation or identity verification. While legit DDNS hostnames are rarely accessed in every-day use, their malicious counterparts are heavily used in phishing campaigns — e.g. paypal‑login.duckdns.org.
ddns.disclaimer: If you are using DDNS, note that this setting will not block the DDNS services' own website or their update API.

parked.name: Block Parked Domains
parked.description: Parked domains are single-page websites often laden with ads and devoid of any value. Parked domain monetization can sometimes get mixed up with suspicious practices and malicious content.

tld.name: Block Top-Level Domains (TLDs)
tld.description: Block all domains and subdomains belonging to specific TLDs.

csam.name: Block Child Sexual Abuse Material
csam.description: Block domains hosting child sexual abuse material with the help of Project Arachnid, operated by the Canadian Centre for Child Protection. No information is transmitted back to Project Arachnid when a domain is blocked.

blocklists.name: Blocklists
blocklists.description: Block ads & trackers using the most popular blocklists available — all updated in real-time.

nextdns-recommended.name: NextDNS Ads & Trackers Blocklist
nextdns-recommended.description: A comprehensive blocklist to block ads & trackers in all countries. This is the recommended starter blocklist.

native.name: Native Tracking Protection
native.description: Block wide spectrum trackers — often operating at the operating system level — that track your activity on a device. This could include all the websites you visit, everything you type or your location at all times.

disguised.name: Block Disguised Third-Party Trackers
disguised.description: Automatically detect and block third-party trackers disguising themselves as first-party to circumvent recent browser's privacy protections like ITP.

affiliate.name: Allow Affiliate & Tracking Links
affiliate.description: Allow affiliate & tracking domains common on deals websites, in emails or in search results. Those usually only get called after manually clicking on a link.
affiliate.relay: Your IP address will automatically be hidden from those websites to preserve your privacy.

services.name: Websites, Apps & Games
services.description: Restrict access to specific websites, apps and games.
services.add: Add a website, app or game

categories.name: Categories
categories.description: Restrict access to specific categories of websites and apps.

gaming.name: Online Gaming
gaming.description: Blocks online gaming websites, online gaming apps and online gaming networks (Xbox Live, PlayStation Network, etc.).

piracy.name: Piracy
piracy.description: Blocks P2P websites, protocols, copyright-infringing streaming websites and generic video hosting websites used mainly for illegally distributing copyrighted content.

porn.name: Porn
porn.description: Blocks adult and pornographic content. It includes escort sites, pornhub.com and similar domains.

social-networks.name: Social Networks
social-networks.description: Blocks all social networks sites and apps (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, Reddit, etc.). Does not block messaging apps.

video-streaming.name: Video Streaming
video-streaming.description: Blocks video streaming services (YouTube, Netflix, Disney+, illegal streaming websites, video porn websites, etc.) and video-based social networks (TikTok, etc.). This can also help in reducing bandwidth usage on any network.

recreation.name: Recreation Time
recreation.description: Set a period for each day of the week during which some of the websites, apps, games or categories above will not be blocked — e.g. allow Facebook on Mondays and Tuesdays between 6pm and 8pm.
recreation.howto: Use the <1></1> button in the sections above to enable Recreation Time.

safesearch.name: SafeSearch
safesearch.description: Filter explicit results on all major search engines, including images and videos. This will also block access to search engines not supporting this feature.

youtubeRestricted.name: YouTube Restricted Mode
youtubeRestricted.description: Filter out mature videos on YouTube and block embedded mature videos from being watched on other websites. This will also hide all comments.

bypass.name: Block Bypass Methods
bypass.description: Prevent or hinder the use of methods that can help bypass NextDNS filtering on the network. This includes VPNs, proxies, Tor-related software and encrypted DNS providers.

errors.ip: Enter a domain, not an IP address.
errors.tld: It looks like you are trying to block an entire TLD, please use the Block TLDs feature in the Security section.
errors.exists: This domain has already been added.

denylist.info: Denying a domain will automatically deny all its subdomains.

allowlist.info: Allowing a domain will automatically allow all its subdomains. Allowing takes precedence over everything else, including security features.

resolved.name: Resolved Domains
resolved.description: Domains that resolved without being blocked by any setting or because they were manually allowed.

blocked.name: Blocked Domains
blocked.description: Domains blocked by a Security, Privacy and/or Parental Control setting or because they were manually denied.

root.name: Root Domains
root.description: Aggregate of all queries made for root domains and all their subdomains.

reasons.name: Blocked Reasons
reasons.description: Security, Privacy and/or Parental Control settings that blocked the most queries.

gafam.name: GAFAM Dominance
gafam.description: The "GAFAM" (Google, Amazon, Facebook, Apple and Microsoft) are the 5 dominant Internet companies that own many popular services, often operating under a different name, e.g., WhatsApp and Instagram for Facebook.

encrypted.name: Encrypted DNS
encrypted.description: Percentage of queries made using a encrypted transport (DNS-over-HTTPS, DNS-over-TLS or the official NextDNS apps).

dnssec.name: DNSSEC
dnssec.description: Percentage of queries validated with DNSSEC.

destination.name: Traffic Destination
destination.description: Countries where your Internet traffic goes.

advertising.description: Provides advertising or advertising-related services such as data collection, behavioral analysis or retargeting.

comments.description: Enables comments sections for articles and product reviews.

customer_interaction.description: Includes chat, email messaging, customer support, and other interaction tools.

essential.description: Includes tag managers, privacy notices, and technologies that are critical to the functionality of a website.

adult_advertsing.description: Delivers advertisements that generally appear on adult content sites.

site_analytics.description: Collects and analyzes data related to site usage and performance.

social_media.description: Integrates features related to social media sites.

audio_video_player.description: Enables websites to publish, distribute, and optimize video and audio content.

cdn.description: Content delivery network that delivers resources for different site utilities and usually for many different customers.

misc.description: This tracker does not fit in other categories or has not yet been labelled.

pornvertising.description: Delivers advertisements that generally appear on adult content sites.

hosting.description: This is a service used by the content provider or site owner.

extensions.description: Software on user-devices that can intercept and analyse browser traffic (man in the middle).

deviceSelector.howto: Learn how to identify your devices on the Setup page.

name.name: Name
name.description: Pick a name for this profile.

us.name: United States
us.description: Logs will be stored within the United States. This is the default.

eu.name: European Union
eu.description: Logs will be stored within member states of the European Union.

gb.name: United Kingdom
gb.description: Logs will be stored in London, United Kingdom.

ch.name: Switzerland
ch.description: Logs will be stored in Zürich, Switzerland — a jurisdiction known for its strong privacy laws.

confirm.title: Change storage location
confirm.text: Changing the storage location of the logs will delete all past logs and analytics for this profile.
Are you sure you want to clear all the logs for this profile? This action cannot be undone.

blockpage.name: Block Page
blockpage.description: Display a block page when a domain is being blocked. This may slightly increase page load time and an HTTPS warning may appear in some cases. When disabled, blocked queries will be answered with the unspecified address (0.0.0.0 or ::).

ca.name: NextDNS Root CA
ca.description: Remove the HTTPS warning when loading the block page by installing and trusting our root CA at <1>{{link}}</1>. Read instructions on how to do this <3>here</3>.

ecs.name: Anonymized EDNS Client Subnet
ecs.description: Speed up the delivery of data from content delivery networks without exposing your IP address.

cache.name: Cache Boost
cache.description: Minimize DNS queries by enforcing a minimum TTL (Time to live).

flatten.name: CNAME Flattening
flatten.description: Prevent CNAME-chasing resolvers from making unnecessary queries and pollute the logs with intermediate domains.

web3.name: Web3
web3.description: Web3 refers to a decentralized and censorship-resistant online ecosystem comprised of innovative technologies such as blockchain-based domain registries (e.g. Ethereum Name Service) and distributed content storage and delivery networks (e.g. IPFS). When enabled, NextDNS will act as an unfiltered gateway to this new Web, letting you experience it firsthand without the need to install anything.
web3.tips: As most browsers only support classic top-level domains at the moment, you should add a trailing slash ("<1>/</1>") when trying to access a Web3 domain directly (e.g. "<3>vitalik.eth/</3>" instead of "<5>vitalik.eth</5>").

rewrites.name: Rewrites
rewrites.description: Set or override the DNS response for any domain. Rewrites apply to subdomains as well, and local IP addresses are supported as answers.

errors.ip: Enter a domain, not an IP address.
errors.taken: You already have a rewrite for that domain.

errors.invalid: Answer should be a valid IP address or domain.

access.name: Access
access.description: Provide editing or viewing-only access to this profile to others.

errors.duplicate: This email has already been invited.

duplicate.description: Copy all settings of this profile to a new one.

errors.taken: You already have a profile with that name.

delete.warning: This will delete this profile and all the logs associated with it forever.

confirm.text: Are you sure you want to delete <1>{{name}}</1>? This action cannot be undone.

leave.warning: You will lose access to this profile.

confirm.text: Are you sure you want to leave <1>{{name}}</1>? This action cannot be undone.
